behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (dispatcher -> three reviewer clients), not pure single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network behavior is required to detect wrong payload routing. LLM-DEP: assertion is about request construction, not model judgment. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is "sends ... instruction pack" (transport correctness only), not output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real dispatch/orchestration modules with fake reviewer adapters that capture calls; assert each role receives identical document bytes and its own instruction pack (no cross-role swap).

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model extraction quality; deterministic stubs hide missed/incorrect terms. MULTI-STEP: single reviewer decision, not a multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text is only the clause extractor’s own decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause extractor model on representative contracts with gold annotations; score recall/precision for parties, dates, obligations, penalties and fail below threshold.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears when policy config is transformed and handed from orchestrator/config layer to risk-assessor client. REAL-INFRA: does not require real network/OS to detect malformed/missing policy fields. LLM-DEP: does not assert model judgment, only invocation payload. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is "configuration is passed correctly".
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestration with real config loading/mapping modules and a capturing risk-assessor stub; assert final invocation contains exact policy IDs/thresholds/flags.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: detecting indirect/euphemistic risk language depends on model semantic judgment; mocks will systematically miss real failure modes. MULTI-STEP: single reviewer output quality check, not sequential agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one reviewer’s classification quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real risk assessor with policy and a labeled corpus containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses; evaluate detection metrics and policy-consistent severity labeling.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs in cross-module parameter handoff (profile selection/mapping -> compliance call). REAL-INFRA: can be caught without real HTTP/OS primitives. LLM-DEP: does not depend on compliance model output, only call inputs. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is limited to "passed correctly ... invocation".
behaviors.test_description: Run real profile resolution plus invocation builder with a capturing compliance-checker adapter; assert jurisdiction code and regulatory profile fields match configured values.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is model decision correctness against regulatory requirements; stubbed responses would hide misclassification/omission. MULTI-STEP: still one reviewer’s decision boundary. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text evaluates only compliance checker correctness, not cross-agent sequence effects.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real compliance checker on contracts labeled for multiple jurisdictions/regimes; verify pass/fail decision and requirement-level rationale coverage against ground truth.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: conflict preservation depends on how reviewer outputs (potentially different schemas/IDs) are combined, a module-boundary interaction in merger pipeline. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP checks: no real OS/network needed; no dependence on model judgment if using fixed reviewer outputs; not a multi-agent decision sequence test. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is about merge policy ("preserves both assessments with attribution"), not whether reviewers judged correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merger with realistic extractor/risk/compliance output objects (including same-clause conflict) through actual normalization + merge modules; assert unified report keeps both labels and source attribution.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (near-tie Unit vs Integration): a pure unit test of merge function can miss failures where upstream normalization maps clause IDs differently per reviewer, causing dropped conflicts before merge logic runs.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: requires sequential/parallel agentic stages (three live reviewer decisions + downstream merge). LLM-DEP: each reviewer output is model-dependent; correctness of "without losing findings" depends on real outputs passing through full pipeline. REAL-INFRA may be present, but workflow is the tighter minimum boundary because failure can emerge from cross-step agentic interactions and handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on real contracts using live clause/risk/compliance reviewers; verify final merged report contains every finding from each reviewer output with correct attribution and no dropped clauses.
